briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — aggressive pre-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Trim the skirt and add a narrow shad profile soft plastic trailer to improve visual attraction and hooking percentages; bass take the bait deeper and hold on longer than with a skirt alone
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...
Wheeler: At Santee Cooper, pre-spawn keys were 10 ft and shallower, hard places and rock (hard bottom, rock, gravel), plus brush and stumps. Better if you had both rock and wood but didn't need both
Kevin Hawk: Owner Flashy accent (Indiana or willow blade on barrel swivel slipped onto hook bend) used in murky water or high pressure situations to draw more attention to wacky-rigged Senko

Palaniuk: 'I call it floating a crankbait -- pick a crankbait that dives deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel it so it crawls up and over rocks, then back down the other side. It looks like a crawfish slowly scooting.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
